message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Las Casuelas Nuevas (Rancho Mirage)
Cuisine/type signal: Mexican / hacienda-style
Why it surfaced in discovery: Cornerstone of Restaurant Row established 1973 by Del and Mary Delgado; first Spanish colonial hacienda-style Mexican dinner house in the country; over 50 years operating; recipes date back generations; hosted Sinatra, Eastwood, Streisand
Discovery sources: https://www.lascasuelasnuevas.com/our-history | https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/family-traditions-celebrate-40-years-at-las-casuelas-nuevas/ | https://thedesertinsider.com/las-casuelas-nuevas-where-history-heritage-and-flavor-live-on/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

I've found reviews for Las Casuelas Nuevas in Rancho Mirage from 2026. Here's what the current reviews show:

## Overview

Las Casuelas Nuevas is an authentic Mexican food restaurant in Rancho Mirage, established in 1973. It has won awards for Best Mexican Restaurant, Best Outdoor (Patio) Dining, and Best Sunday Brunch.

## Yelp Reviews

The Yelp page has 693 photos and 1,071 reviews, with recent updates as of June 2026.

## Positive Feedback

Most reviews are highly positive. Las Casuelas Nuevas is highly recommended for its great food, beautiful setting, and welcoming atmosphere, with great atmosphere, wonderful service, and fantastic food. The restaurant is praised for its high-quality food, including the best salsa and tableside guacamole.

Visitors mention making annual trips to see the singer Geronimo and enjoy great Mexican food with fabulous entertainment at a decent price point.

## Mixed Reviews

However, there are some critical reviews. One reviewer reported the food was poor, with tacos that were soaked in oil and soggy rather than crisp.

## Atmosphere

The restaurant is impressive, built in the style of a Mexican hacienda, with architecture that transports visitors, a beautifully done outdoor courtyard, and a cavernous inside seating area.

- Florencio "Del" Delgado (founder, with wife Mary)

- Family recipes originated in 1900s Mazatlán, Mexico
- "The tradition began in the 1900s in the culturally rich city of Mazatlán"
- Original restaurant established in 1958 with multi-generational recipes

- Patio inspired by San Miguel de Allende architecture

**Awards & Recognition:**

- Via Florencio street dedication recognizing Del's "vision and faith in Rancho Mirage"

70-050 Highway 111, Rancho Mirage, CA 92270

**Contact:** (760) 328-8844

**Hours:** Not specified on page

**Menu Structure:** Not detailed; online ordering available

**Schema.org Markup:** Not visible in provided content; restaurant information present but structured data not extracted

Las Casuelas Nuevas is the proud winner of the Best of the Valley for Best Mexican Restaurant, Best Outdoor (Patio) Dining, and Best Sunday Brunch. Additionally, readers voted for their favorite businesses across the Coachella Valley in more than 20 categories for the 2022 Best of the Best competition by Palm Springs Life magazine, and Las Casuelas won two prizes in reader voting.

# Las Casuelas Nuevas - Restaurant Summary

**Rating & Ranking:**
- 4.1 out of 5 stars
- #2 of 80 restaurants in Rancho Mirage
- 747 total reviews

**Awards:**
- Travelers' Choice award (consistently earns great reviews and ranks in top 10% on Tripadvisor)

**Recent Reviews (2025-2026):**

The restaurant has received mixed feedback recently. Positive comments highlight the atmosphere and food quality. One visitor praised "the best thin crispy chips in the Coachella Valley" and noted "excellent dinner" experiences. Another reviewer celebrated the "beautiful" venue and mentioned staff excellence for private events.

However, negative reviews raised concerns about food quality and service. One diner complained the food was "overpriced and tasteless," while another described tacos as "soaked in oil" and "not crisp, but soggy." A third reviewer noted the restaurant lacks modern payment systems, specifically the inability to "split a check by items," which frustrated group dining experiences.

**Key Themes:** Atmosphere consistently praised; portions generous; guacamole preparation debated; service quality varies; pricing questioned by some diners.

content.content:
# Las Casuelas Nuevas: Machine-Readable Data Assessment

## Structured Data Findings

**Limited Schema.org Implementation**: The webpage lacks comprehensive JSON-LD markup or microdata that would enable search engines to automatically parse key restaurant information.

## Available Machine-Readable Signals

**Present:**
- Contact phone: 760-328-8844
- Physical address clearly displayed: 70-050 Highway 111, Rancho Mirage, CA 92270
- Operating hours provided in plain text format
- Direct links to reservation and online ordering systems

**Missing:**
- No JSON-LD Restaurant schema
- No OpeningHoursSpecification markup
- No LocalBusiness schema with structured address/phone
- Menu data exists but only as PDF and external ordering system links, not embedded schema
- No aggregateRating or review schema connected to TripAdvisor/social citations

## Social Media & External Links

The site references presence on Instagram, Facebook, and TripAdvisor but doesn't embed verification markup (rel="me" or equivalent).

## Overall Assessment

The website prioritizes human readability over machine readability. While basic contact and hours information is accessible, the absence of standardized schema markup limits discoverability through voice search, rich snippets, and automated aggregation services.

**Top Recommendation: Las Casuelas Nuevas**

Las Casuelas Nuevas is a local culinary institution and a staple of Restaurant Row that has stood the test of time serving great Mexican food in the Coachella Valley for over 50 years. The Delgado family has been serving their matriarch's recipes for more than 60 years, and the Rancho restaurant delights guests with its Spanish hacienda architecture, tasty margaritas, and menu items like tableside guacamole.

**Other Top Options in Rancho Mirage:**

Best Mexican in Rancho Mirage includes Las Casuelas Nuevas, Los Arcos Mexican Restaurant, Casa Luna Mexican & Seafood Restaurant, and Guadalajara Cafe & Bar. Los Arcos Mexican Restaurants has a location at 72817 Dinah Shore Dr, Ste 103 in Rancho Mirage.

**Additional Coachella Valley Options:**

The Coachella Valley has several other highly-rated authentic Mexican restaurants worth exploring, including a family-owned restaurant in Coachella on Sixth Street that offers favorites like street tacos, quesabirria, and chiles rellenos.

Links: [{"title":"Las Casuelas Nuevas | The Desert Insider","url":"https://thedesertinsider.com/las-casuelas-nuevas-3/"},{"title":"Las Casuelas Nuevas: Where History, Heritage, and Flavor Live On | The Desert Insider","url":"https://thedesertinsider.com/las-casuelas-nuevas-where-history-heritage-and-flavor-live-on/"},{"title":"Our History | Mexican Food | California | Las Casuelas Nuevas","url":"https://www.lascasuelasnuevas.com/our-history"},{"title":"Las Casuelas Original | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/las-casuelas-original-palm-springs/"},{"title":"About us | Las Casuelas Terraza","url":"https://www.lascasuelas.com/about-us"},{"title":"21 The Delgado Family ideas | palm springs, palm springs california, rancho mirage","url":"https://www.pinterest.de/lcnuevas/the-delgado-family/"},{"title":"Las Casuelas Original - Palm Springs Mexican Restaurants and Bars","url":"https://directory.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/las-casuelas-original/"},{"title":"Mary Delgado Obituary (2007) - Palm Springs, CA - The Desert Sun","url":"https://www.legacy.com/us/obituaries/thedesertsun/name/mary-delgado-obituary?id=24310350"}]

Here's what I found about Las Casuelas Nuevas:

## History and Family Legacy

Las Casuelas Nuevas's story begins in the culturally rich city of Mazatlán, Mexico, where founder Florencio "Del" Delgado's grandmother crafted and perfected family recipes that have since become the heart of this beloved establishment. The Delgado family's culinary journey took root in 1958 with the opening of the Original Las Casuelas, where Mary and "Del" delighted patrons with dishes passed down through generations.

## The Restaurant

By 1973, Las Casuelas Nuevas had become a cornerstone of the Coachella Valley dining scene, attracting the glitterati of Hollywood and beyond. It was the first Spanish Colonial hacienda-style restaurant in the nation, designed by renowned architect Arthur Valdes, with the building constructed with handmade bricks, roof tiles, and wrought iron railings, all carefully sourced from Mexico.

## Celebrity History

The restaurant has hosted legends like Frank Sinatra, Clint Eastwood, Barbara Streisand, Gregory Peck, and Dolly Parton, and other luminaries. From the golden era of Frank Sinatra and Gregory Peck to modern icons like the Kardashians, this desert gem has drawn stars under its roof for decades, with legendary musician Lalo Guerrero even gracing its stage.

## Recognition

In 2006, the city of Palm Springs honored Mary Delgado by shining a star with her name on the city's walk of fame, and the street which runs next to the restaurant was dedicated as Via Florencio, citing his "vision and faith in Rancho Mirage and for starting Restaurant Row."

Here are the search results for the best restaurants in Coachella Valley's Restaurant Row in Rancho Mirage:

## Restaurant Row - Rancho Mirage Anchor Restaurants

Las Casuelas Nuevas is a local culinary institution and a staple of Restaurant Row that has stood the test of time serving great Mexican food in the Coachella Valley for over 50 years.

## Top-Rated Restaurants in Rancho Mirage

**Upscale Dining:**

- The Edge Steakhouse at the Ritz-Carlton features elevated views and offers an unparalleled dining experience.

- Shame on the Moon has been a fine-dining date night hot spot since 1984.

- Willie's Modern Fare offers classic cuisine with a modern twist, complemented by inventive cocktails and a thoughtfully curated wine list.

**Diverse Cuisine Options:**

- Foxy's Kitchen + Bar is a family-run spot where steaks and osso buco are favorites, complemented by an array of seafood, pastas, and vegan options.

- Kobe Japanese Steakhouse, founded in 1978, offers a warm and entertaining environment with highly trained teppan chefs serving the finest ingredients.

- Shabu Shabu Zen offers a cozy and fun Japanese dining experience with premium Wagyu beef and fresh seafood, earning a spot on Yelp's "100 Best Places to Eat" list in 2017.

Rancho Mirage is a dining destination that caters to every taste, offering everything from upscale steakhouses to casual, family-friendly spots and lively brewpubs.

content.text: I have sufficient evidence. Let me compile the structured assessment.

input.name: Las Casuelas Nuevas
input.cuisine_or_type: Mexican / Spanish colonial hacienda-style dinner house

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 1,071 reviews + TripAdvisor 747 reviews; Google [UNVERIFIED but likely 2,000+]
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews (last 6-12 months) consistently praise atmosphere, tableside guacamole, salsa/chips, live entertainment (singer Geronimo), and Spanish colonial hacienda setting. Recurring criticisms: occasional food execution complaints (soggy/oily tacos), 'overpriced and tasteless' from a minority, inability to split checks by item frustrates groups. TripAdvisor rating 4.1/5, ranked #2 of 80 restaurants in Rancho Mirage.
reputation.longevity: Founded 1973 by Florencio 'Del' and Mary Delgado; over 50 years in continuous operation by the Delgado family. Family's restaurant lineage in the valley goes back to original Las Casuelas in 1958 (Palm Springs). Ownership stable and multi-generational.
credentials: Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' reader-vote winner — Best Mexican Restaurant, Best Outdoor/Patio Dining, Best Sunday Brunch (multi-year, including 2022)
TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice; ranked #2 of 80 restaurants in Rancho Mirage
Mary Delgado star on Palm Springs Walk of Stars (2006)
Street adjacent to restaurant dedicated as 'Via Florencio' honoring Del Delgado's role in founding Restaurant Row
Featured in Palm Springs Life, The Desert Insider, Visit Greater Palm Springs

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'best Mexican restaurant Rancho Mirage', 'date night Rancho Mirage', 'hacienda Mexican Coachella Valley', 'Restaurant Row Rancho Mirage', 'authentic Mexican Greater Palm Springs', 'Mexican Sunday brunch Coachella Valley'. Cited by Visit Greater Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life, TripAdvisor, Yelp, Locale Magazine, AvantStay, Wanderlog as a Rancho Mirage anchor. Less likely to surface for broader 'best fine dining Palm Desert' or El Paseo-anchored queries.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Accurate. Agent summaries correctly identify: 1973 founding, Delgado family ownership, Spanish colonial hacienda architecture, Restaurant Row cornerstone status, 50+ year operation, celebrity history. Some sources conflate the original 1958 Palm Springs Las Casuelas with the Nuevas 1973 opening date, but the family lineage is correctly described.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Weak. lascasuelasnuevas.com presents address (70-050 Highway 111, Rancho Mirage), phone (760-328-8844), hours, reservation and online ordering links in plain HTML, but lacks JSON-LD Restaurant schema, OpeningHoursSpecification, LocalBusiness schema, aggregateRating, or embedded menu schema. Menus exist as PDFs and external ordering links, not machine-parseable structured data. Site is human-readable but agent-unfriendly relative to reputation.

competitors: Las Casuelas Terraza (downtown Palm Springs — sister/family-related concept on same lineage)
Los Arcos Mexican Restaurant (Rancho Mirage, 72817 Dinah Shore Dr)
Casa Luna Mexican & Seafood Restaurant (Rancho Mirage)
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — explicitly credited as the founding restaurant of Rancho Mirage's Restaurant Row (Highway 111); Via Florencio street dedication marks Del Delgado as the corridor's originator
query_wins: date night Mexican Rancho Mirage with patio and live music
historic celebrity Mexican restaurant Palm Springs area

convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Owns a real branded website (lascasuelasnuevas.com) with online ordering and reservation integrations, active Instagram and Facebook presence, paid/listed presence across Yelp, TripAdvisor, OpenTable, Visit Greater Palm Springs, GAYOT, and local press features (Palm Springs Life, The Desert Insider, Locale Magazine). Hosts live entertainment (Geronimo) suggesting ongoing programming budget. No visible agency footprint, but consistent multi-channel presence indicates in-house or contracted marketing.
convertibility.reasoning: Independently owned and operated by the Delgado family across multiple generations — no chain, franchise, or resort parent. A local decision-maker is reachable (family stewardship continues; founder's descendants run operations). The site lacks schema.org structured data despite high reputation, leaving a concrete, demonstrable agent-visibility gap that a services pitch can close. Strong reputation, real but agent-unfriendly digital footprint, and clear marketing-spend signals (paid listings, events, social) make this a textbook convertible target rather than an anchor-reference.

flags: Google review count not directly verified — Yelp shows 1,071 and TripAdvisor 747, but exact Google Maps review band is [UNVERIFIED]

Founding-year sourcing inconsistency: discovery brief and most third-party sources say 1973 for Las Casuelas Nuevas; official site references 1958 as start of the broader Las Casuelas family tradition (Original Las Casuelas in Palm Springs). Nuevas itself = 1973.

Architect attribution conflict: official site credits Raul Sosa for 1983 patio; secondary source attributes original hacienda design to Arthur Valdes — [UNVERIFIED] which architect designed which portion

Celebrity guest claims (Sinatra, Eastwood, Streisand, Peck, Parton, Kardashians, Lalo Guerrero) repeated across the restaurant's own site and friendly press but not independently documented — treat as marketing claim
Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' wins referenced in third-party summaries; specific year-by-year roster beyond 2022 not individually verified [UNVERIFIED]
No schema.org / JSON-LD structured data on the restaurant's own website — concrete agent-visibility gap
